description:
South Suburban Ice Arena, located at 6901 South Peoria Street in Centennial, Colorado, operates as a modern public ice skating facility managed by the South Suburban Parks and Recreation District. This single-sheet rink serves the rapidly growing communities of Centennial, Littleton, and the south Denver metropolitan suburbs with comprehensive programming including public skating, figure skating instruction, and youth and adult ice hockey development. Conveniently positioned near the intersection of Interstate 25 and C-470, South Suburban Ice Arena provides accessible ice skating services to Colorado's booming south suburban population seeking quality recreational facilities.

The facility's programming emphasizes accessibility and community development, offering public skating sessions at affordable district rates throughout the week and weekend. Youth hockey leagues develop young ice hockey talent within the south Denver suburbs, while learn-to-skate programs introduce beginning skaters to foundational techniques in welcoming community settings. Adult recreational hockey leagues provide play opportunities for working adults seeking team competition and social engagement. Figure skating instruction and club programming serve skaters of various competitive levels, providing coaching pathways from beginner through advanced amateur competition.

South Suburban Ice Arena's location in Centennial positions it at the heart of the south Denver suburbs, an area experiencing significant population growth and increasing ice sports participation. The facility's commitment to affordable public programming and strong youth development makes it a valuable community asset. With convenient access from I-25 and C-470 and ample on-site parking, the arena welcomes families from throughout the south metro Denver region seeking quality ice skating at reasonable prices.
what_to_know: Check the South Suburban Parks and Recreation District website for current public skating schedules, as hours vary seasonally
The facility is a single sheet; hockey leagues and programs may occasionally affect public skating availability
Skate rentals are available at reasonable rates; bring your own skates if you prefer better fit
Learn-to-skate programs for children and adults are popular; register early during fall and winter enrollment periods
Youth hockey league registration typically occurs in late summer; check the Parks and Recreation District website for tryout dates
Ample parking is available on-site; the location is easily accessible from I-25 and C-470
Figure skating coaching may be available through partnerships or independent coaches; inquire at the facility for coaching options

faq.question: What is the South Suburban Parks and Recreation District?
faq.answer: The South Suburban Parks and Recreation District is the public agency managing South Suburban Ice Arena and other recreational facilities in Centennial, Littleton, and south Denver suburbs. The district provides affordable public programming and community-focused ice skating services.

faq.question: Are public skating rates affordable at South Suburban Ice Arena?
faq.answer: Yes, South Suburban Ice Arena operates under the South Suburban Parks and Recreation District with affordable public ice skating rates designed to make recreational skating accessible to all families. Check the district website for current pricing and any resident discounts.

faq.question: What hockey programming is available for youth at South Suburban Ice Arena?
faq.answer: South Suburban Ice Arena hosts youth ice hockey leagues providing recreational and competitive opportunities for young players. Register through the Parks and Recreation District; registration typically opens in late summer, with team assignments and schedules announced before the season begins.

faq.question: How do I get to South Suburban Ice Arena from downtown Denver?
faq.answer: South Suburban Ice Arena is located at 6901 South Peoria Street in Centennial, easily accessible via Interstate 25 south or C-470. The drive from downtown Denver takes approximately 20-30 minutes depending on traffic and parking is readily available on-site.

faq.question: Are learn-to-skate classes available for all ages?
faq.answer: Yes, South Suburban Ice Arena offers learn-to-skate programs for children and adults in various age groups and skill levels. Check the Parks and Recreation District website for current class schedules, instructor information, and registration dates.

description:
Foothills Ice Arena, located in the Littleton and Englewood area of Colorado at 3901 West Floyd Avenue, serves as a community ice skating facility operated by the Foothills Park and Recreation District. This single-sheet rink provides public skating, figure skating instruction and clubs, and youth and adult ice hockey programming to the residents of Littleton, Englewood, and the southwest Denver metropolitan region. The facility's location in the scenic foothills area offers the added appeal of mountain views and an outdoor recreation atmosphere that complements the ice skating experience.

The facility emphasizes family engagement and recreational accessibility, offering public skating sessions at affordable district rates throughout the week and weekend. Learn-to-skate programs serve beginning skaters of all ages, providing foundational instruction in supportive community settings. Youth hockey leagues develop ice hockey talent within the Littleton and Englewood communities, fostering both recreational and competitive play opportunities for young athletes. Figure skating coaching and club programming serve skaters of various skill levels, with instruction available from beginner through advanced amateur competition.

Foothills Ice Arena's location in the foothills southwest of Denver provides convenient access to the growing communities of Littleton and Englewood while maintaining the scenic mountain atmosphere characteristic of the region. The facility's commitment to affordable public programming and community development makes it a preferred choice for southwest Denver families. The dramatic mountain backdrop adds a unique appeal to the ice skating experience, distinguishing it from other metro Denver facilities and making it a special destination for recreational skaters.

The facility is a single sheet; hockey programming may occasionally affect public skating times

faq.answer: Foothills Ice Arena is located at 3901 West Floyd Avenue in the Littleton/Englewood area, operated by the Foothills Park and Recreation District. It serves both communities and the broader southwest Denver metropolitan region.

faq.question: What makes Foothills Ice Arena unique?
faq.answer: Foothills Ice Arena's location in the Colorado foothills provides spectacular mountain views and a scenic backdrop to ice skating, distinguishing it from other metro Denver facilities. The natural setting combined with quality ice skating programming makes it a special destination.

faq.answer: Foothills Ice Arena hosts youth ice hockey leagues and learn-to-play programs for children of various ages and skill levels. Registration typically occurs in late summer; check the Parks and Recreation District website for current tryout dates, team placements, and league schedules.

description:
Edge Ice Arena, located at 9315 Dorchester Street in Highlands Ranch, Colorado, operates as a premier ice skating facility serving the large and affluent Highlands Ranch master-planned community and surrounding south suburban Denver region. This two-sheet facility represents one of Colorado's busier ice arenas, with comprehensive programming including public skating, figure skating instruction and competitive coaching, and extensive youth and adult ice hockey development. Operated by the Highlands Ranch Community Association (HRCA), Edge Ice Arena has established itself as a premier destination for serious ice hockey talent development and figure skating instruction throughout the south Denver suburbs.

The facility's two sheets of ice enable simultaneous hosting of multiple programs, tournaments, and diverse skating activities throughout the day and week. Public skating sessions provide recreational opportunities for casual skaters and families, while figure skating clubs and coaching programs develop competitive skaters through structured instruction and pathway programs. Youth and adult hockey leagues span recreational through elite competitive levels, attracting serious hockey players from throughout the metro Denver region and establishing the facility as a hub for hockey talent development. The rink's reputation for excellence in hockey and figure skating programming draws athletes from communities well beyond Highlands Ranch.

Edge Ice Arena's location in Highlands Ranch, one of Colorado's largest and most established master-planned communities, positions it at the center of an affluent suburban region with strong sports participation and community engagement. The facility's two-sheet capacity, professional coaching staff, and competitive programming make it one of the more sophisticated ice skating operations in Colorado. Whether you're seeking recreational public skating, serious competitive coaching, or youth athletic development, Edge Ice Arena provides comprehensive programming and professional facilities.
what_to_know: Visit hrca.net or call the facility for current public skating schedules, as two sheets host simultaneous programming
The two-sheet facility hosts hockey tournaments and competitions; check the website for event schedules and spectator information
Skate rentals are available; bring your own skates if you prefer better fit and performance
Figure skating coaching is available through competitive pathways; inquire about lesson levels and club memberships
Youth hockey programming spans recreational through elite competitive levels; registration varies by program level
Ample parking is available; the facility is easily accessible from local Highlands Ranch roads
The facility attracts serious hockey and figure skating athletes from throughout the metro Denver region

faq.question: How many sheets of ice does Edge Ice Arena have?
faq.answer: Edge Ice Arena operates two sheets of ice, making it one of Colorado's busier ice skating facilities. The two sheets allow simultaneous hosting of public skating, figure skating coaching, hockey leagues, and tournaments throughout the day.

faq.question: Is Edge Ice Arena only for Highlands Ranch residents?
faq.answer: While operated by the Highlands Ranch Community Association for the master-planned community, Edge Ice Arena welcomes skaters from throughout the south Denver suburbs. The facility's two-sheet capacity and comprehensive programming attract serious hockey and figure skating athletes from across the metro Denver region.

faq.question: What hockey tournaments does Edge Ice Arena host?
faq.answer: Edge Ice Arena hosts youth and adult hockey tournaments and competitions throughout the season. Check hrca.net or call the facility for current tournament schedules, entry information, and spectator details.

faq.question: What figure skating programs are available?
faq.answer: Edge Ice Arena hosts figure skating clubs and offers competitive coaching instruction for figure skaters of various levels. Contact the facility or visit hrca.net to learn about coaching availability, lesson options, and club membership information.

faq.question: How do I register for hockey at Edge Ice Arena?
faq.answer: Hockey registration at Edge Ice Arena varies by program level and age group. Contact the facility or visit hrca.net for current registration dates, team information, tryout schedules, and league structure details.

description:
Family Sports Center, situated at 13451 East Briarwood Avenue in Centennial, Colorado, operates as a multi-sport complex featuring two sheets of ice within a comprehensive athletic facility. Located in Arapahoe County, the facility serves the residents of Centennial, Aurora, and the southeast Denver suburban communities with extensive public skating, figure skating instruction and clubs, and competitive youth and adult ice hockey programming. As one of Denver metro's busier hockey facilities, Family Sports Center has earned a strong reputation for serious hockey talent development and comprehensive ice skating services to a wide regional population.

The facility's two sheets of ice enable simultaneous hosting of multiple programs, leagues, and skating activities, accommodating the high demand for ice sports in the rapidly expanding southeast Denver suburbs. Public skating sessions provide recreational opportunities for casual skaters and families seeking affordable recreation, while figure skating clubs and coaching instruction serve skaters pursuing competitive pathways. Youth and adult hockey leagues span recreational through elite competitive levels, with serious competitive programming attracting talented hockey players from throughout the metro Denver region. The facility's integrated sports complex model provides additional amenities and programming beyond ice skating.

Family Sports Center's location in Centennial positions it at the heart of southeast Denver's growing suburban communities. The facility's reputation for hockey excellence, coupled with its two-sheet capacity and comprehensive programming, makes it a destination facility for serious ice hockey athletes throughout Colorado. The multi-sport complex approach provides convenient access to diverse athletic programming for families balancing multiple children's sports interests and recreational needs.

description:
Apex Ice Arena, located at 13150 Huron Street in Westminster, Colorado, operates as an affordable public ice skating facility managed by the Apex Park and Recreation District. This single-sheet rink serves the communities of Westminster, Broomfield, and the north Denver suburbs with comprehensive programming including public skating at district rates, figure skating instruction and clubs, and youth and adult ice hockey leagues. Conveniently positioned near US-36, Apex Ice Arena provides accessible ice skating services to Colorado's booming northern suburbs at prices designed to make ice sports affordable for all families.

The facility emphasizes accessibility and community participation, offering public skating sessions throughout the week and weekend at affordable district rates. Learn-to-skate programs introduce beginning skaters of all ages to fundamental techniques in supportive community environments. Adult and youth hockey leagues develop ice hockey talent within the Westminster and Broomfield communities, with programming spanning recreational through competitive levels. Figure skating coaching and instruction serve skaters pursuing competitive pathways, with club programming available for serious figure skating athletes.

Apex Ice Arena's location in Westminster positions it as a central hub for ice skating in the north Denver suburbs. The facility's commitment to affordable public programming through the Apex Park and Recreation District makes quality ice skating accessible to families throughout the region. With convenient access from US-36 and ample on-site parking, the arena welcomes skaters from Westminster, Broomfield, and surrounding north suburban communities seeking affordable, quality ice skating programming.

description:

Serving the communities of Littleton, Ken Caryl, and southwest Jefferson County, Ice Ranch has established a strong reputation for comprehensive programming including public skating, figure skating clubs and competitive coaching, and youth and adult ice hockey leagues. The two-sheet facility's private operation model emphasizes quality programming and professional coaching services, attracting serious skating athletes from throughout the southwest Denver metropolitan region.

The facility's two sheets of ice enable simultaneous hosting of public skating, figure skating training, competitive hockey leagues, and special events throughout the week. Figure skating clubs operate on-site with access to experienced coaching staff, providing training pathways from beginner through competitive levels. Youth and adult hockey leagues span recreational through elite competitive programming, with the facility establishing itself as a serious hockey destination in southwest Denver. Learn-to-skate programs serve beginning skaters of all ages, providing foundational instruction in welcoming community settings.

Ice Ranch's location in Littleton positions it at the heart of southwest Denver's growing communities. The facility's private operation model allows flexibility in programming and the ability to attract high-quality coaching staff and professional programming. The two-sheet capacity accommodates the high demand for ice sports in the affluent southwest suburbs, while the facility's reputation for excellence in figure skating and hockey coaching makes it a preferred destination for serious skating athletes throughout Colorado.

description:
Lone Tree Recreation Center Ice Rink, located at 10249 RidgeGate Circle in Lone Tree, Colorado, operates as a modern ice skating facility within a full-service recreation center complex opened in 2012. This single-sheet rink serves the City of Lone Tree, Parker, and the south Douglas County communities with comprehensive programming including public skating, figure skating instruction and clubs, and youth and adult ice hockey leagues. As part of a larger recreation center offering diverse athletic and wellness services, the ice rink represents a modern, community-focused approach to recreational ice sports programming in Colorado's rapidly growing south suburbs.

The facility's single ice sheet hosts diverse programming throughout the day and week, including public skating sessions designed for families and casual skaters seeking affordable recreation. Figure skating instruction and club programming serve skaters of various skill levels, with coaching available for those pursuing competitive pathways. Youth and adult hockey leagues develop ice hockey talent within the Lone Tree and Parker communities, with programming spanning recreational through competitive levels. The integration of the ice rink within a comprehensive recreation center provides additional amenities and programming options for families and serious athletes.

Lone Tree Recreation Center's location in Lone Tree, a planned community in south Douglas County experiencing significant growth, positions it as a modern recreational hub for the region. The facility's opening in 2012 reflects the area's strong commitment to quality athletic and recreational programming. The ice rink's integration within a full recreation center complex creates a convenient, comprehensive recreational destination for families balancing multiple athletic interests. Modern facilities and professional programming make this an attractive option for south Douglas County residents.

description:
Broadmoor Ice Palace, located at 1 Lake Avenue in Colorado Springs, Colorado, stands as one of Colorado's most storied and historic ice skating facilities. Adjacent to the legendary Broadmoor resort, this prestigious rink represents a cornerstone of Colorado ice sports history, having produced multiple Olympic champions including Carol Heiss and Dorothy Hamill, who trained at this facility to achieve international skating greatness. The single-sheet Ice Palace combines public skating access with elite-level figure skating coaching and instruction, offering visitors the opportunity to skate on the same ice that has hosted Olympic champions and trained generations of competitive skaters.

Broadmoor Ice Palace's reputation for figure skating excellence extends from its historic production of Olympic champions through its contemporary commitment to serious competitive coaching and instruction. The facility offers public skating sessions allowing recreational skaters and families to experience the same ice surface used by international champions. Elite coaching staff provide instruction and training pathways for serious figure skaters pursuing competitive success at regional, national, and international levels. The resort setting and proximity to downtown Colorado Springs create a distinctive experience combining ice skating with the legendary Broadmoor resort atmosphere.

Located in the stunning Rocky Mountain setting near downtown Colorado Springs, Broadmoor Ice Palace provides an exceptional ice skating experience distinguished by its historic significance, Olympic legacy, and premium resort setting. The dramatic mountain backdrop, proximity to the World Arena and other Colorado Springs attractions, and the facility's Olympic heritage make it a destination for serious figure skaters and visitors seeking to experience skating at a legendary venue. Whether training competitively or enjoying recreational public skating, Broadmoor Ice Palace offers a uniquely prestigious Colorado ice sports experience.
what_to_know: Visit broadmoor.com/activities/ice-skating for current public skating schedules and hours
The facility is located adjacent to the Broadmoor resort; premium pricing reflects the resort setting and historic venue
Elite figure skating coaching is available; contact the facility for coaching availability, lesson costs, and competitive pathways
Public skating sessions offer the opportunity to skate on Olympic champion ice; arrive early as sessions may fill during peak times
The World Arena, home of the U.S. Figure Skating Championships, is located nearby; the area is a major figure skating destination
Ample parking and resort amenities are available; the location offers access to world-class dining and accommodations
The facility is historically significant; Olympic champions Carol Heiss and Dorothy Hamill trained here to achieve international success

faq.question: What Olympic champions trained at Broadmoor Ice Palace?
faq.answer: Broadmoor Ice Palace is historically significant as the training facility for Olympic champions including Carol Heiss and Dorothy Hamill. These world-renowned figure skaters developed their skills and trained at this facility to achieve Olympic and international success.

faq.question: Is Broadmoor Ice Palace part of the Broadmoor resort?
faq.answer: Broadmoor Ice Palace is located at 1 Lake Avenue adjacent to the legendary Broadmoor resort in Colorado Springs. While operated as an ice skating facility, it benefits from the resort's premium atmosphere, amenities, and dining services.

faq.question: What figure skating coaching is available?
faq.answer: Broadmoor Ice Palace offers elite-level figure skating coaching and instruction for skaters pursuing competitive success. Contact the facility at broadmoor.com or by phone for coaching availability, lesson costs, competitive pathways, and elite training program information.

faq.question: Can I do public skating at Broadmoor Ice Palace?
faq.answer: Yes, Broadmoor Ice Palace welcomes the public for recreational skating sessions. Visit broadmoor.com/activities/ice-skating for current public skating schedules, hours, and admission pricing. Sessions may fill during peak times; check ahead for availability.

faq.question: Why is Broadmoor Ice Palace historically important?
faq.answer: Broadmoor Ice Palace is one of Colorado's most storied ice skating facilities, having trained Olympic champions and contributed significantly to Colorado's ice sports legacy. The facility's historic significance, combined with its contemporary elite coaching programs, makes it a prestigious venue in American figure skating.
